title: agrostis stolonifera subvar. malcuitiana
intro: Agrostis stolonifera subvar. malcuitiana, also known as Creeping Bentgrass, is a perennial grass species characterized by its creeping stems and stolons. It is reported to have medicinal properties, with its leaves and roots containing compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antiseptic activities. The health benefits of Creeping Bentgrass include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Creeping Bentgrass can be made by infusing its leaves and roots in hot water or oil to create teas, tinctures, and salves, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential applications.
